question: Choose the correct word: 'We left the drink in the freezer and soon it became ________ — hard and no longer liquid.' (not hollow, completely hard)
choices: solid
hollow
curved
spherical

question: Choose the correct word: 'Decorative designs on the thaob may feature plants, ________ shapes or birds.' (having regular lines or shapes)
choices: embroidered
precious
geometric
hollow

question: Choose the correct word: 'The silk is ________ with threads of wool or cotton.' (decorated with sewn patterns)
choices: dyed
embroidered
woven
carved

question: Choose the correct word: 'Traditionally, the threads were coloured with natural ________ from plants.' (things used to change cloth colour)
choices: silks
threads
jewels
dyes

question: 'Modern clothes designers want to keep the ________ of the thaob alive by using traditional embroidery designs in other products.' Choose the correct vocabulary word.
choices: heritage
dye
thread
cast

question: Which sentence correctly uses the phrase 'in suspense'?
choices: She was completely satisfied, so she waited in suspense
He had already seen the film, so he sat in suspense
I have been in suspense all week, waiting to see what happens next!
They were very calm and relaxed, in suspense about the result

question: Which sentence correctly uses 'under the impression'?
choices: She was under the impression of the teacher because he was so smart
The audience was under the impression to watch the whole show
I was under the impression that the show started at 7, but it was at 8
He made a good impression because he was under the impression

question: Which of the following sentences correctly uses 'in addition to'?
choices: She enjoyed the story in addition to the end
He left in addition to the cinema on time
They went to the theatre in addition to the restaurant
In addition to her acting role, she also sang in the show

question: Choose the correct past modal to complete: 'The holes in the ancient flute _____ man-made because they fit a right-handed musician perfectly.'
choices: must have been
might have been
can't have been
wouldn't have been

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'It is possible that this was a candle holder used in ancient ceremonies.'
choices: This must have been a candle holder
This can't have been a candle holder
This might have been a candle holder
This would be a candle holder

question: Choose the correct past modal: 'It can't just have been a coincidence.' What does this sentence express?
choices: a possibility — it could have been a coincidence
an assumption — the speaker thinks it was a coincidence
a logical conclusion — it was probably a coincidence
a negative deduction — the speaker is sure it was not a coincidence

question: Which of the following correctly uses 'would have' to express a logical conclusion?
choices: The Isle of Lewis belonged to Norway, so the merchant would have been in his own country when he lost the chess pieces
The merchant would have been there if he wasn't in Norway
He would have the chess pieces if he had been careful
Norway would have many chess pieces from the merchant

question: Choose the correct past modal continuous form: 'I can't have been ________ when I tripped and fell.'
choices: looking where I was going
pay attention
paying attention
to look where I was going

question: An amazing discovery at Petra in Jordan _____ if archaeologists hadn't used a satellite to study the ancient city. Choose the correct form.
choices: might never have discovered
might never have been discovered
might have never discovered
might never been discovered

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'I had been standing for hours. I felt very tired.'
choices: Not standing for hours, I felt very tired
Standing for hours, I felt very tired
To stand for hours, I felt very tired
Having been standing for hours, I felt very tired

question: Choose the correct reduced form: 'Cameron won an award and got the best actor prize.'
choices: Cameron won an award, also getting the best actor prize
Having won an award, Cameron got the best actor prize
Cameron won an award, to get the best actor prize
Cameron won an award and getting the best actor prize

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The opera house was decorated in a very ________ way — elegant and tasteful throughout.' (decorated well)
choices: tasty
lively
alive
tasteful

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'The artist's best work has a ________ innocence about it.' (positive quality natural to a child)
choices: childish
comical
childlike
insipid

question: Choose the correct confused word: 'If only the artist had been ________ to see his painting sell at auction.' (still living)
choices: lively
classic
alive
historical

question: The phrase 'be on the edge of your seat' means ________.
choices: to be so tired you can barely stay awake
to be confused and unable to follow the story
to feel bored and distracted by a performance
to be in suspense and fully engaged with what is happening

question: The word 'dialogue' in the context of theatre or film refers to ________.
choices: the conversation between characters in a play or film
the list of actors who appear in a performance
the set of costumes worn during a production
the storyline that connects all scenes in a film

question: The word 'costume' in the context of theatre refers to ________.
choices: the background scenery on the stage during a performance
the music played during the most important scenes
the special clothing worn by actors during a performance
the written words spoken by characters in a play

question: The phrase 'play the lead' means to ________.
choices: take a small supporting role in a production
direct the performance from behind the scenes
play the most important main character in the production
act as a narrator who introduces the story

question: Choose the correct word to complete: 'My friend is always cracking ________ jokes that aren't at all funny.' (sentimental and of poor quality)
choices: offensive
exceptional
appalling
cheesy

question: The adjective 'appalling' used to describe a performance means ________.
choices: far better than any other performance in the show
extremely funny and difficult to stop laughing at
very offensive because of its language and content
shockingly bad and of very poor quality

question: The phrase 'The plot is quite complex' in a review is used to ________.
choices: describe how the lead actor behaves in the main scene
tell the reader about the main costume designs used
give information about the storyline of the production
explain why the performance was not enjoyable

question: In a review article, 'Far from being...' is used to ________.
choices: agree strongly with a previous argument or statement
summarise the main points in the conclusion
give a concrete example to support an argument
make a suggestion to the reader about what to do next

question: What is the most important idea in the passage about resilience?
choices: Resilient people do not experience stress or difficulties in their lives
Only naturally gifted people can develop resilience over time
Resilience is built through difficulty, and everyone can learn to be more resilient
People who are upset by stress should avoid difficult situations

question: What advice does the author give about problems in the building resilience article?
choices: Give up quickly if the problem seems too difficult to solve
Ask for help from someone else before trying to solve it yourself
Ignore the problem and wait for it to resolve itself naturally
See the problem as a challenge and trust that you will overcome it

question: According to information about Jordan's constitutional system, the 'constitution' is ________.
choices: the group of important ministers who control government policy
the process by which new laws are debated and passed
the first version of a proposed law submitted to parliament
the principles and ideas by which a country agrees to be governed

question: According to information about Jordan, the current Parliament building has been in use since ________.
According to information about world parliaments, the world's largest parliament is China's. It has ________ members.
According to information about Jordan's parliamentary process, the Prime Minister's proposals are FIRST referred to ________.
choices: the House of Deputies
the King for approval
the Senate for review
the Ministry of the Interior

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', a 'verse' is defined as ________.
choices: the final line of a poem that sums up the main idea
a repetition of the same sound at the end of two lines
the use of the same letter at the beginning of nearby words
a group of lines that form one part of a poem

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', the phrase 'To watch his woods fill up with snow' shows that the speaker is stopping ________.
choices: because he is lost and cannot find the road
because he is afraid of the frozen lake nearby
to watch the snow fall in the beautiful forest
to look for shelter from the cold winter storm

question: In the poem 'Stopping by Woods on a Snowy Evening', which of the following is an example of alliteration?
choices: Between the woods and frozen lake
The darkest evening of the year
His house is in the village though
To watch his woods fill up with snow

question: According to the text, people who are less emotionally connected with characters ________.
choices: are happy to know what will happen because it requires less effort
refuse to read any stories even if they are given spoilers
always avoid spoilers because they dislike knowing the ending
are more likely to read stories more than once for enjoyment

question: In the context of Jordan's parliament, a 'draft bill' is ________.
choices: a final law that has been approved by both houses of parliament
a letter written by the King to the Prime Minister
the first version of a proposed law
a report submitted by the Senate to the House of Deputies

question: In the writing section on articles, the introduction should ________.
choices: list the main themes of the book in alphabetical order
provide a detailed description of the main characters
summarise the plot from the beginning to the very end
grab the reader's attention and outline what the article is about

question: Which of the following correctly uses 'would it be OK if...?' to make a suggestion?
choices: Would it be OK if we saw a traditional food display instead?
Would it be OK if we will see a traditional food display instead?
Would it be OK if we have seen a traditional food display instead?
Would it be OK to we see a traditional food display instead?
